Short Description:
West Valley Medical Center is a 117-bed acute care hospital located in Caldwell, Idaho. It serves the Canyon County community by providing advanced, patient-centered healthcare across various specialties, including cardiology, oncology, orthopedics, and emergency services. The hospital is recognized as an Accredited Chest Pain Center and a Level III Trauma Center, and it has received a five-star quality rating from CMS.

The facility handles over 44,000 outpatient visits and 35,000 emergency room visits annually. It features a 13-bed ICU, advanced operating rooms with robotic surgery capabilities, and a range of imaging services, including CT and MRI. West Valley Medical Center emphasizes a compassionate approach to care through its "We Care Model," which focuses on personalized patient interactions. The hospital offers comprehensive services, including emergency care, cancer treatment, spine care, and women's health, ensuring a wide array of healthcare options for its patients.

Short Description:
Strive Health is a leader in value-based kidney care, focusing on patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD) and end-stage kidney disease (ESKD). The company collaborates with healthcare payors, providers, and health systems to enhance kidney care through early patient engagement and comprehensive coordinated care. Strive emphasizes data-driven insights and prevention to improve patient outcomes while reducing costs.

The company offers a turn-key kidney care service line that includes the CareMultiplier™ platform, which utilizes machine learning to help clinicians develop personalized care plans. Strive also features nurse practitioner-led care teams known as The Kidney Heroes™, along with accredited case management and population health programs. With a significant clinical impact, Strive has achieved notable reductions in hospital admissions and overall care costs.

Strive serves over 145,000 individuals with kidney disease and partners with more than 6,500 providers across all 50 states. The company has received multiple workplace awards and is backed by prominent investors, securing $550 million in recent funding to further its mission in transforming kidney care.

Short Description:
Dental365 is a dentist-founded and dentist-led Dental Clinical Organization (DCO) that offers a wide range of general and specialty dental services across the northeastern United States. Established in 2014, the company has grown to operate locations in New York, Connecticut, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Ohio, New Hampshire, Massachusetts, and Rhode Island. With a team of 1,001-5,000 employees, Dental365 is headquartered in the New York metropolitan area.

The organization focuses on providing high-quality dental care through a collaborative network of experienced dentists and specialists. Services include general dentistry, oral surgery, cosmetic dentistry, orthodontics, endodontics, and emergency dentistry. Dental365 is committed to preventive care and enhancing patients' quality of life, utilizing advanced technology and evidence-based practices. The company also supports dental practices with management resources and continuing education through its "Dental365 University" program.

Short Description:
Dental Associates is Wisconsin's largest family-owned dental group practice, offering comprehensive family dental care since its founding in 1974. The company started with a single clinic in Milwaukee and has expanded to 14 locations across Eastern Wisconsin, from Green Bay to Kenosha. With over 800 team members, including more than 90 doctors, Dental Associates operates 629 operatories and is led by Dr. Thomas Manos.

The practice provides a full range of dental services, including general dentistry, pediatric dentistry, orthodontics, dental implants, cosmetic dentistry, periodontics, endodontics, and specialized care for older adults. Dental Associates emphasizes team dentistry, allowing patients to receive care from in-house specialists without needing to seek external referrals. The company accepts over 200 insurance plans and offers financing options, including the exclusive CarePlus Dental Plans. Their mission focuses on improving patients' quality of life through excellence in oral health care.

Short Description:
Thirty Madison, Inc. is a telehealth platform that connects patients with independent, physician-owned medical groups to facilitate clinical telehealth services. The company operates under several brands, including Keeps, Cove, Facet, and Nurx, which focus on specific health conditions. Through its platforms, U.S.-based providers offer consultations, secure messaging, and related care via telemedicine.

While Thirty Madison does not provide medical advice or services directly, it partners with KMG Medical Group and its affiliates to deliver professional medical care. The company offers self-pay options and accepts certain private insurance plans, assisting with claims submission as a courtesy. It also manages the fulfillment of prescriptions through affiliated pharmacies, ensuring a streamlined process for patients. Thirty Madison prioritizes privacy and compliance, handling protected health information in accordance with regulations.

Short Description:
EnableComp is a technology-driven company that specializes in complex revenue cycle management (RCM) solutions for healthcare providers, health systems, hospitals, and ambulatory surgery centers across the United States. With over 24 years of experience, the company processes more than 21 million claims and has recovered over $5 billion in revenue. Headquartered in Franklin, Tennessee, EnableComp employs around 201 people and generates annual revenue of approximately $187.9 million.

The company's core offering is the E360 RCM™ intelligent automation platform, which streamlines the entire claim lifecycle, from payer verification to electronic billing and appeals for underpayments. EnableComp focuses on complex claims, including those related to Veterans Administration, Workers’ Compensation, and Motor Vehicle Accidents. The platform is designed to minimize denials and delays while maximizing revenue yield. EnableComp serves over 1,000 clients nationwide, emphasizing a people-first culture and flexible work environment, and has received accolades such as #1 Specialty RCM Solution by Black Book in 2024.

Short Description:
Rx Savings Solutions (RxSS) is a healthcare technology company founded in 2008, focused on reducing prescription drug costs through its web-based software. The company, which is now part of McKesson Corporation, serves over 18 million members and analyzes 175 million pharmacy claims each year. RxSS partners with more than 60 Fortune and Global 500 clients, including Select Health, to enhance clinical transparency and provide personalized savings suggestions.

The company's mission is to empower consumers by simplifying pharmacy benefits and improving medication adherence, ultimately lowering out-of-pocket costs. RxSS offers a patented software platform that combines technology, engagement, and concierge support to identify savings opportunities beyond traditional methods. Key features include real-time pricing accuracy, intelligent notifications, and personalized guidance from pharmacists. This approach not only helps members save on medications but also enables employers and health plans to manage their pharmacy spending effectively.

Short Description:
DrFirst, Inc. is a healthcare technology company based in Rockville, Maryland, founded in 2000 by James F. Chen. The company specializes in medication management, e-prescribing, and care collaboration solutions aimed at enhancing patient outcomes across interconnected healthcare systems. With a workforce of around 500 employees, DrFirst generates approximately $105 million in revenue and serves a vast network that includes 420,000 prescribers, 2,000 hospitals and health systems, and 71,000 pharmacies, impacting over 100 million patients annually.

DrFirst offers a range of products and services designed to improve workflow efficiency and data access. Their flagship product, Rcopia, is a widely used e-prescribing system that processes over 2 million prescriptions each month. Other offerings include medication management tools, care collaboration solutions, and innovative features like AI-powered fraud alerts and real-time benefit checks. DrFirst emphasizes interoperability through partnerships with nearly 100 EMR/EHR vendors and has received multiple awards for its contributions to the healthcare IT sector.

Short Description:
GoodRx Holdings, Inc. is a healthcare technology company based in Santa Monica, California, founded in 2011. The company aims to enhance healthcare accessibility and affordability by providing price transparency for prescription drugs. GoodRx allows users to compare prices from local pharmacies and offers discounts, reportedly saving Americans over $20 billion since its inception.

The core services include a website and mobile app that enable real-time searches for the lowest prescription prices at over 70,000 pharmacies across the U.S. Users can access digital coupons and discount cards without needing insurance. GoodRx has also expanded its offerings through telehealth services, allowing for medication refills and virtual consultations, particularly after acquiring HeyDoctor. The company continues to grow, serving 17 million monthly users and focusing on making prescription medications more affordable.

AMC Health is a virtual care and digital health company founded in 2002 by Nesim Bildirici. Headquartered in New York City, the company focuses on remote patient monitoring and telehealth solutions to assist individuals in managing chronic conditions from home. AMC Health aims to help people lead healthier lives by providing proactive insights and clinical support.

The company offers a range of services, including chronic disease management programs that utilize Bluetooth monitoring devices to transmit biometric data to healthcare providers. They also provide telemonitoring for conditions such as COPD and congestive heart failure, as well as diabetes telehealth programs. AMC Health's virtual care monitoring services combine biometric data with voice response information to enhance participatory care.

Under the leadership of Founder & CEO Nesim Bildirici and a team of experienced executives, AMC Health has achieved significant clinical outcomes, including reduced patient A1c levels and decreased hospital readmission risks.

Helping Hands Family (HHF) is a prominent provider of evidence-based Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) therapy, specializing in autism services for children aged 2 to 6 years. The company operates 29 service locations across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Maryland, Connecticut, Virginia, and New York. HHF is dedicated to delivering individualized, play-based therapy that enhances communication, social skills, focus, and learning readiness.

Founded with a mission to provide compassionate, family-focused support, HHF employs data-driven methods to track progress and adapt treatment plans. The company has received notable accreditations, including a 100% rating from the Behavioral Health Center of Excellence and Great Place to Work™ certification for four consecutive years. In November 2024, HHF expanded its reach by acquiring Mission Autism Clinics, adding 12 locations in the Northeast US.

HHF offers customized ABA therapy in various settings, including in-clinic, in-home, and in-school. Key programs include the Little Hands Day Program, an alternative to traditional preschool for children with autism. The therapy approach emphasizes positive reinforcement and child-led sessions, creating a nurturing environment that fosters growth and development.

Quadax, Inc. is a healthcare services and information technology company based in Cleveland, Ohio, founded in 1973. With over 50 years of experience, Quadax specializes in revenue cycle management (RCM) solutions that enhance financial performance for healthcare providers. The company employs around 1,100 people and generates approximately $268.1 million in revenue, focusing on client-centered innovation.

Quadax offers a range of RCM services and technologies, including claims management, reimbursement support, denial management, and patient access solutions. Their AI-powered platform, iQ: Intelligence by Quadax, automates workflows and provides real-time insights to improve cash flow and accuracy. The company also provides SMARTedi™, a claims solution for behavioral health. Quadax serves various healthcare organizations, including hospitals, laboratories, and physician groups, and has established partnerships with entities like State of Ohio Medicaid and Streamline Healthcare Solutions.

Revele is a healthcare revenue cycle management (RCM) company that specializes in optimizing revenue cycles and implementing electronic health records (EHR) for physician groups, health systems, and medical practices. Founded in 1999 and headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana, Revele also has a subsidiary in India for back-office operations. The company employs between 51 and 200 people and focuses on enhancing revenue, reducing costs, and improving EHR utilization through data-driven technology and strategic partnerships.

Revele offers a wide range of services, including full outsourcing of revenue cycle management, EHR services, clinical coding, healthcare analytics, and professional services. Their solutions are designed to support HIPAA compliance and improve operational efficiencies. The company emphasizes innovation and aims to increase cash flow and collections for its clients. Revele partners with various healthcare providers and organizations, including MD Revolution, to enhance patient care and practice revenue.

Athelas is a healthcare technology company based in Mountain View, California, founded in 2014 or 2015 by Tanay Tandon and Deepika Bodapati. The company focuses on AI-driven solutions for remote patient monitoring (RPM), revenue cycle management (RCM), electronic health records (EHR), and practice management. Athelas aims to simplify operations, enhance revenue, and improve patient care across the United States.

The company offers an integrated platform featuring three core products: Athelas RCM, Ambient AI, and EHR, along with hardware like Athelas Home. Their RPM solutions include FDA-cleared devices for monitoring vital signs and chronic disease management, while their RCM tools leverage AI to optimize billing processes and improve financial outcomes for healthcare providers. Athelas also provides Ambient AI for documentation support and an all-in-one EHR system that streamlines patient engagement and operational workflows. With a strong focus on innovation, Athelas has processed billions in payments and achieved significant growth in patient engagement.

DECA Dental Group is a Dallas-based dental service organization founded in 2008 by Dr. Sulman Ahmed. The company operates nearly 200 affiliated practices across nine states under the Ideal Dental brand, providing high-quality and affordable dental care. DECA manages all operational aspects for its network, allowing dentists to concentrate on patient care. The organization employs around 2,000 people and has reported approximately $87 million in revenue.

DECA offers a wide range of comprehensive dental services, including preventative care, general dentistry, cosmetic treatments, restorative procedures, pediatric dentistry, and orthodontic care. The company emphasizes a patient-centric culture and aims to be the premier provider of dental services while supporting dentists and staff in their professional growth. DECA is also committed to community involvement through partnerships with various charities. The organization continues to expand its presence across the United States, focusing on key regions like Texas, California, and Illinois.

Amwell, also known as American Well Corporation, is a prominent telehealth company based in Boston, Massachusetts. Founded in 2006, it offers a comprehensive digital healthcare platform that connects patients with healthcare providers for virtual, in-person, and automated care. The company emphasizes a HIPAA-compliant ecosystem that enhances clinician experiences and patient access across various care settings.

The flagship platform, Amwell Converge, supports a hybrid care model that includes urgent care, chronic disease management, behavioral health, and wellness consultations. Amwell serves over 100 million members and collaborates with more than 50 health plans and 2,000 healthcare partners. Key features of the platform include telemedicine modalities for on-demand consultations, hybrid care integration with advanced technology solutions, and automated tools for improved patient engagement. Amwell also provides tailored solutions for healthcare providers, payers, and employers, ensuring efficient care delivery across the United States.

CareSpot Urgent Care is a healthcare provider focused on urgent care services, operating primarily in Florida and Tennessee. Founded in 2001 and headquartered in Brentwood, Tennessee, the company employs around 660 people and generates annual revenue of approximately $176.2 million. CareSpot became a division of United Surgical Partners International (USPI) in 2016.

The company offers a wide range of urgent care services for non-emergency illnesses and minor injuries. Their services include general urgent care treatment, on-site laboratory work, X-ray capabilities, prescription filling, wellness testing, and occupational health services. CareSpot enhances patient experience through technology, providing online appointment scheduling, digital check-in, and post-visit communications. With extended hours, including holidays and weekends, CareSpot is recognized as the largest urgent care provider in Florida and partners with various healthcare systems.

Short Description:
Texas MedClinic is a network of walk-in urgent care clinics based in San Antonio, Texas. Founded in 1982 by Dr. Bernard Swift, the company operates 20 locations throughout South and Central Texas, including cities like Austin and New Braunfels. In 2022, Texas MedClinic was acquired by Community Care Partners.

The clinics specialize in urgent care, occupational medicine, and physical therapy services. They provide treatment for non-life-threatening medical emergencies, work-related injuries, and rehabilitation services. Texas MedClinic operates on a walk-in basis, with many locations offering 24-hour service. The company performs over 500,000 patient visits annually and employs a team of experienced medical professionals, including approximately 70 independent contractor physicians. Their target customers include individual patients, employers, and construction companies seeking occupational health services.

First Choice Emergency Room (FCER) is the largest freestanding emergency room system in the United States, established in 2002 in Dallas, Texas. Owned by Adeptus Health, FCER provides an efficient alternative to traditional hospital emergency rooms. The facilities are equipped to handle both adult and pediatric emergencies, featuring advanced diagnostic technology such as CT scanners, ultrasound, digital X-ray, and on-site labs.

FCER is known for its high patient satisfaction, ranking in the 98th percentile nationally according to Press Ganey feedback. All locations are staffed by board-certified emergency physicians and emergency-trained registered nurses. The company operates multiple facilities across Texas and Colorado, with additional locations in Arizona. FCER focuses on delivering comprehensive emergency care while ensuring a seamless patient experience through its Patient Journey model.

Omada Health is a virtual-first healthcare company based in San Francisco, California, founded in 2011. The company specializes in evidence-based digital solutions for managing chronic conditions through behavior change science, personalized coaching, and integrated care platforms. It began as a project at IDEO, focusing on translating medical literature into engaging digital products, particularly for prediabetes.

Omada offers a comprehensive platform that includes customized care pathways for long-term health management. Its key programs address prediabetes, diabetes, hypertension, musculoskeletal issues, and behavioral health. The platform features virtual healthcare services, one-on-one coaching, digital tools, and data-driven insights. Omada serves over 1,700 employer and health plan customers, providing coverage to more than 1 million members. The company emphasizes a multidisciplinary approach, combining clinical practices and data science to improve health outcomes.

Short Description:
Welldoc is a digital health company based in Columbia, Maryland, founded in 2005 by an endocrinologist. The company focuses on improving chronic disease management, particularly for diabetes, through AI-driven platforms and mobile technology. Welldoc specializes in healthcare technology that integrates personalized, real-time insights into patients' daily lives, enhancing self-management and health outcomes for conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, heart failure, obesity, and behavioral health.

The Welldoc Platform is a scalable digital coaching solution that supports multi-condition management and enables "care anywhere" models for health plans, systems, and employers. Its flagship product, BlueStar®, is an FDA-cleared prescription digital therapeutic for adults with type 2 diabetes, providing in-app coaching and real-time guidance. Welldoc collaborates with various partners, including Samsung Health and LifeScan, to extend care and optimize outcomes for patients. The company has received multiple FDA clearances and has a strong emphasis on evidence-based solutions.

ConvenientMD is a walk-in and virtual healthcare provider based in Portsmouth, New Hampshire. Founded in 2012, the company operates 26 urgent care clinics across New Hampshire, Maine, and Massachusetts, focusing on suburban and rural areas that lack primary care options. With around 1,146 employees, ConvenientMD generates $63 million in annual revenue and is a Certified B Corporation, reflecting its commitment to social impact alongside profitability.

The company offers a wide range of healthcare services, including injury and illness care, diagnostic imaging, lab services, minor surgical procedures, and occupational health services. ConvenientMD also provides telehealth options, allowing patients to access virtual care and consultations with specialists. Their mission is to make healthcare more convenient for all, serving individual patients, employers, and underserved communities with a focus on affordability and quality care. Each clinic is staffed by a team of medical professionals equipped with advanced technologies to ensure effective treatment.

Home Care Delivered (HCD) is a direct-to-patient provider of insurance-covered medical supplies for chronic health conditions. Founded in 1996 by Gordy Fox and based in Glen Allen, Virginia, HCD delivers name-brand products to patients' homes within 48 hours. The company simplifies access to medical supplies by managing insurance claims, paperwork, and reordering for individuals, caregivers, healthcare providers, and insurers.

HCD serves over 34,000–40,000 customers nationwide, primarily Medicare and Medicaid enrollees. The company specializes in a range of disposable home medical supplies, including diabetes management products, incontinence care items, urology supplies, ostomy systems, and wound care dressings. HCD also offers patient compliance support, prescription coordination, and expertise in insurance coverage to ensure customers receive the supplies they need. With a focus on compassion, reliability, and compliance, HCD utilizes proprietary technology for efficient claims processing and order monitoring.

Next Level Urgent Care, also known as Next Level Medical, is a Texas-based urgent care provider with over 45 clinics in cities such as Houston, Beaumont, Austin, and San Antonio. Founded by Dr. Juliet Breeze, the company aims to offer affordable and high-quality care for non-life-threatening illnesses and injuries. Their services are designed to be faster and significantly less expensive than emergency room visits, with average copays ranging from $25 to $100 for insured patients and transparent pricing for uninsured patients.

The clinics are open daily from 9 AM to 9 PM and provide 24/7 virtual care. They treat patients of all ages for a variety of conditions, including general urgent care, dermatology, optometry, and gynecology. Facilities are equipped with on-site X-ray equipment and skilled providers, ensuring patient comfort and care. Next Level Urgent Care is the first in Texas to receive Urgent Care Center Accreditation from the American Academy of Urgent Care Medicine, highlighting their commitment to high standards and positive health outcomes.

LifeLong Medical Care is a community health center organization based in Berkeley, California, serving the Bay Area. It focuses on providing comprehensive primary care, behavioral health, and supportive services to underserved populations, including the homeless, elderly, and individuals with chronic conditions. The organization operates across 11 sites in cities like Berkeley, Oakland, and Richmond, emphasizing accessible and compassionate care.

The services offered include primary care, specialty care, and dental services. LifeLong provides family practice, pediatric care, women's health, and street medicine, along with HIV care and geriatric services. Their dental clinics cater to both the general population and the homeless through mobile units. Additionally, LifeLong integrates behavioral health services, offering individual and family counseling, as well as community programs focused on chronic disease management and health education.

LifeLong also addresses housing needs through its supportive housing program, which includes on-site primary care at Single Room Occupancy (SRO) sites. The organization is dedicated to promoting overall wellness and connecting patients to essential services in a supportive environment.

Sonoma Valley Hospital (SVH) is a full-service acute care district hospital located in the city of Sonoma, California, providing compassionate expert care to the 42,000 residents of the Sonoma Valley Health Care District. The Hospital has 24 acute care beds and maintains a 27-bed Skilled Nursing Facility. SVH is the sole local provider of acute inpatient care and offers a 24-hour emergency room, inpatient services with an ICU, surgical services and outpatient clinical testing and treatment.

In 2018, the hospital announced affiliation with UCSF Health. The hospital is fully accredited by the Center for Improvement in Healthcare Quality (CIHQ), meeting national standards for healthcare quality and safety.

CompleteCare Health Network (CCHN) is a nonprofit, federally qualified health center based in Bridgeton, New Jersey. Established in the early 1970s, CCHN has grown to become one of the largest community health organizations in southern New Jersey, serving over 60,000 patients annually through a network of 18–20 patient-centered locations. The organization is dedicated to providing accessible, high-quality healthcare to underserved populations across Gloucester, Cumberland, and Cape May Counties.

CCHN offers a wide range of services, including primary care, women's health, dental care, behavioral health, and specialty services. The organization emphasizes patient-centered care and health equity, utilizing modern technologies and team-based models to enhance the patient experience. CCHN is committed to making healthcare accessible to all, regardless of income or insurance status, and actively supports uninsured or underinsured patients through its sliding-fee program. With a focus on community health improvement, CCHN partners with various entities to further its mission of improving lives through comprehensive healthcare.

LabConnect is a global provider of central laboratory services, established in 2002 and based in Seattle, Washington. The company specializes in technology-driven support for clinical trials, covering all phases from preclinical to Phase IV. With over 20 years of experience, LabConnect has facilitated more than 2,000 clinical studies across 93 countries, collaborating with biopharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, contract research organizations (CROs), foundations, and NGOs.

LabConnect offers a comprehensive range of services, including Central Laboratory Services and Functional Service Provider (FSP) Solutions. Their Central Lab Services encompass routine testing, global sample management, logistics, and data management. The FSP Solutions include scientific project management, regulatory management, and customized staffing solutions. The company emphasizes agility and scalability, providing tailored services for complex studies in areas such as oncology, vaccines, and cell and gene therapies. LabConnect is committed to connecting high-quality samples to accurate data through innovative technology and a customer-focused approach.

Catapult Health is a preventive healthcare company based in Dallas, Texas. It specializes in delivering accessible at-home and worksite health checkups through its VirtualCheckup service. This service targets employers and health plans to enhance employee health, identify risks early, and lower healthcare costs. Catapult Health serves over 3,500 employers and more than 3 million covered lives, achieving high customer satisfaction with an 81+ Net Promoter Score.

Founded by David Michel, Catapult Health focuses on corporate health risks such as diabetes, hypertension, and heart disease. Its VirtualCheckup combines at-home testing kits with real-time video consultations from licensed nurse practitioners, creating personalized health action plans. The service has led to significant outcomes, including a 10% reduction in Metabolic Syndrome among patients and substantial claims savings over three years. Catapult Health operates within Teladoc Health's Integrated Care segment following its acquisition in February 2025.

The Puyallup Tribal Health Authority (PTHA) is a multispecialty clinic established in the 1970s by the Puyallup Tribe of Indians. It aims to provide quality healthcare to the Puyallup Tribe and other Native Americans in Pierce County, Washington. PTHA serves over 300 tribes and has a patient population of more than 13,000 American Indians and Alaska Natives. The clinic operates as an accredited Patient Centered Medical Home.

PTHA was the first Tribal ambulatory health clinic in the U.S. to enter a "638 Self-Determination" contract with the Indian Health Service in 1976. Key facilities include the Takopid Health Center, which offers a range of medical, dental, and pharmacy services, and the Kwawachee building, which focuses on behavioral health and traditional healing. PTHA is committed to promoting wellness in a culturally appropriate manner and employs over 250 staff members. It provides various health services, including medical, dental, pharmacy, and behavioral health, to eligible patients in the region.

The company specializes in advanced medical imaging, diagnostic services, surgical care, and specialty pharmacy solutions. With over 50 locations across New England, Shields Health operates the largest network of AI-enabled MRI machines in the region and has pioneered significant advancements in medical imaging, including Massachusetts' first freestanding MRI center.

The company emphasizes patient-centered care, innovation, and affordability. Its services include MRI and PET/CT scans, ambulatory surgical services, and specialty pharmacy management through Shields Health Solutions. Shields Health partners with over 80 health systems nationwide, enhancing patient outcomes and access to care. Rehabilitation Hospital of Indiana (RHI) is a specialized medical rehabilitation facility located in Indianapolis, Indiana. Established in 1989, RHI provides comprehensive inpatient and outpatient rehabilitation services aimed at restoring physical, cognitive, and psychosocial functions after acute illness or trauma. The hospital operates 91 staffed beds and has five locations across Indiana, focusing on adults with life-changing conditions.

RHI offers a wide range of rehabilitation services, including acute inpatient care, outpatient therapy, and vocational rehabilitation. The interdisciplinary teams consist of physiatrists, neuropsychologists, nurses, and various therapists who work together to create personalized treatment plans. RHI is recognized as the only Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) Model System in Indiana and is noted for its leadership in patient care and medical research. The hospital has received national recognition for its performance and patient outcomes, ranking #41 among the best rehabilitation hospitals in the U.S. according to U.S. News & World Report.

Cranial Technologies, Inc. is a medical company based in Tempe, Arizona, founded in 1986. It specializes in non-surgical treatment for plagiocephaly, commonly known as flat head syndrome, in infants. The company operates a network of over 100 clinics across the United States, providing dedicated care for families of infants affected by this condition.

The flagship product is the DOC Band®, a custom-made cranial helmet that utilizes Digital Surface Imaging® and lightweight materials for effective head reshaping. Cranial Technologies is the sole provider of this FDA-cleared device, having treated over 400,000 babies. The company also offers the CranialTech device, another FDA-cleared option for cranial molding. Services include free initial evaluations, custom helmet design and fitting, ongoing monitoring, and a supportive community for parents.

Short Description:
Shared Medical Services, Inc. (SMS) is a privately-held, employee-owned company based in Cottage Grove, Wisconsin. Founded in 1980, SMS specializes in mobile diagnostic imaging solutions, providing healthcare facilities across the nation with access to advanced imaging technologies without the need for capital investments or construction. The company has grown significantly over its 45-year history, employing around 254 people and generating approximately $27.2 million in revenue.

SMS offers a range of mobile imaging services, including MRI, CT, PET/CT, cardiac PET/CT, and mobile digital mammography. These services are delivered through fully equipped trailers that are deployed directly to hospitals, clinics, and health systems. The company provides both parked and routed mobile imaging options, ensuring consistent access and seamless integration with facility workflows. Additionally, SMS offers interim mobile imaging solutions for short-term needs, maintaining operational continuity during equipment downtimes or upgrades.

MetroSouth Medical Center was a for-profit hospital located in Blue Island, Illinois. It served the south suburban Chicago area and was a key healthcare provider for the local community. Originally opened in 1905, the hospital underwent significant renovations and expansions, with the current facility being built in 1950. By the late 2010s, it had become the largest employer in Blue Island.

Medical Diagnostic Laboratories, L.L.C. (MDL) is a CLIA-certified reference laboratory established in 1997. It specializes in high-complexity, automated DNA-based molecular diagnostic testing, primarily utilizing Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) techniques. MDL focuses on infectious diseases, genetic testing, pharmacogenomics, and cancer diagnostics. The company is headquartered in Hamilton, New Jersey, and is part of the Genesis Biotechnology Group.

MDL offers a range of molecular diagnostics, including testing for bacterial, viral, and fungal infections, as well as genetic and allergy testing. Its unique specimen collection platforms, such as OneSwab®, UroSwab®, and NasoSwab®, facilitate non-invasive and stable transport for high-sensitivity PCR tests. The company emphasizes client retention through customized services and rapid turnaround times, serving laboratories, hospitals, and physicians both in the U.S. and internationally. MDL's research and development team is dedicated to advancing molecular diagnostics and has published findings in peer-reviewed journals.

Palo Pinto General Hospital (PPGH) is the primary countywide provider of healthcare for Palo Pinto, west of Fort Worth, with more than 28,000 residents.

PPGH, based in Mineral Wells, has moved to position itself in our community as more than just an acute care hospital and as a critically needed countywide resource to help foster wellness and combat higher incidences of disease common in rural communities.

Palo Pinto County covers more than 950 square miles, and the county's population is nearly double that of Mineral Wells, PPGH's home base. "With such a spread across the region, we have pushed our services out into the county," Ross Korkmas, PPGH's chief executive officer, said.

In 2021, PPGH opened a family care clinic at Possum Kingdom Lake, the third clinic outside Mineral Wells. PPGH also has family care clinics in Mineral Wells, Gordon, Santo, and an urgent care clinic in Mineral Wells.

Our physicians practice a range of specialties. They include family practice, obstetrics and gynecology, pediatrics, orthopedics, surgery, cardiology, gastroenterology, urology, ophthalmology, anesthesiology, pain management, radiology, infectious diseases, podiatry, critical care, pulmonology, emergency medicine, and hospitalists.

Founded in 1986, the company specializes in advanced diagnostic testing for functional and integrative medicine, focusing on the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of complex chronic diseases. The company offers a variety of tests tailored to functional medicine, including the GI Effects® Comprehensive Stool Profile, NutrEval® Nutritional Assessment, and SIBO Breath Tests. Genova is licensed in several states and is committed to delivering innovative laboratory services using advanced technologies like 16S rRNA PCR and Next-Generation DNA sequencing. Opened in 2003, it was the first hospital in southwest Las Vegas and is part of The Valley Health System, owned by Universal Health Services. The hospital offers a wide range of services, including emergency care, cardiovascular services, maternity and neonatal care, and neurosurgical interventions. It features a Level III NICU, private mother-baby rooms, and various surgical options, including robotic and minimally invasive procedures. Spring Valley Hospital is committed to providing quality care and has received recognition for its services, including awards for being a top place for maternity care. AbsoluteCare is an integrated healthcare provider based in Columbia, Maryland, focused on delivering whole-person care to vulnerable and chronically ill populations. Founded in 2000, the organization has grown from a single care center in Atlanta to multiple locations across several states, primarily serving Medicaid and Dual eligible members. AbsoluteCare operates under a risk-bearing, PCP-driven care model and emphasizes a philosophy called "Beyond Medicine™," which addresses medical, behavioral health, and social needs.

The company offers a wide range of healthcare services, including primary care, infectious disease treatment, behavioral health services, pharmacy with delivery, laboratory and radiology services, infusion services, and social work support. AbsoluteCare employs integrated care teams that work in various settings, including care centers and community locations, to provide comprehensive support. The organization is dedicated to improving healthcare outcomes for its members, demonstrating significant improvements in care visits, hospitalizations, and overall cost of care.

Short Description:
Community Health Alliance Nevada is the largest federally qualified health center in Northern Nevada, providing comprehensive medical, dental, and behavioral health services to over 32,000 patients each year. The organization operates six fixed sites and three mobile units in Reno and Sparks, focusing on underserved and unsheltered populations. It offers affordable care regardless of income or insurance status, utilizing a sliding fee scale and low-cost pharmacies.

The center emphasizes whole-person care, partnering with patients to create personalized health plans. Services include primary care, pediatric care, maternal and prenatal health, dental care, and behavioral health support. Community Health Alliance also provides specialized programs such as HIV care, medication-assisted treatment for substance use, and nutrition services through Women, Infants, and Children (WIC). With extended hours and after-hours support, the organization is dedicated to accessibility and compassionate care for all members of the community.

Cecelia Health is a virtual specialty medical practice based in New York, providing integrated clinical care and chronic condition management services across the United States. Founded in 2009, the company was originally known as Fit4D and rebranded to honor a diabetes educator who significantly impacted the founder's life.

The company offers a range of virtual care services, including integrated specialty medicine, chronic care management, patient support programs, medication adherence solutions, and omnichannel engagement options. Cecelia Health's team consists of registered nurses, dietitians, respiratory therapists, and Certified Diabetes Care & Education Specialists, addressing various chronic conditions such as cardiovascular disease, chronic kidney disease, diabetes, and respiratory conditions. 

Cecelia Health serves health plans, employers, health systems, providers, pharmaceutical manufacturers, and medical device companies, utilizing a white-label approach that allows clients to maintain their brand identity. The company focuses on creating empowering healthcare experiences through strong community connections and innovative solutions.

Kittitas Valley Healthcare (KVH) is a critical access healthcare system located in Kittitas County, Washington. It features a 25-bed hospital in Ellensburg and offers a range of clinics and specialty services throughout Upper and Lower Kittitas County. KVH is dedicated to providing high-quality, patient- and family-centered care, focusing on accessibility and safety.

The KVH Hospital provides comprehensive inpatient and outpatient services, including surgical care, critical care, and a family birthing center. It also offers cardiopulmonary services, laboratory and imaging services, and emergency care. To address community health needs, particularly in women's health, KVH has implemented a hybrid model for obstetrics that includes family practice OBs and certified nurse-midwives.

In addition to hospital services, KVH operates several clinics that provide primary and specialty care, including pediatrics, urgent care, and internal medicine. The organization emphasizes chronic disease management and preventative care, ensuring that the community has access to essential health services. KVH is committed to continuous improvement and quality reporting to enhance patient care.

KidsCare is recognized for its commitment to quality service and has received accolades for its workplace culture and leadership. The agency emphasizes a supportive environment for both its patients and employees, focusing on individualized care that involves family participation for the best outcomes.

MCRA, an IQVIA business, is a prominent Clinical Research Organization (CRO) and consulting advisory firm specializing in medical devices, diagnostics, and biologics. Founded in 2004, MCRA focuses on assisting global healthcare companies in advancing their innovations through all stages of development. The company offers a wide range of integrated services, including regulatory affairs, clinical research, reimbursement consulting, healthcare compliance, quality assurance, and digital health services.

With a team of over 25 former FDA officials and industry experts, MCRA has extensive experience across various therapeutic areas such as orthopedics, cardiovascular, oncology, and digital health. MCRA serves a diverse client base, from medical device startups to large publicly traded companies, with a unique emphasis on neuro-musculoskeletal devices and therapies. Operating globally, MCRA is committed to streamlining the clinical trial process to enhance enrollment and improve FDA approval rates.

Short Description:
GoodRx Holdings, Inc. is a healthcare technology company based in Santa Monica, California, founded in 2011. The company aims to enhance healthcare accessibility and affordability by providing price transparency for prescription drugs. GoodRx allows users to compare prices from local pharmacies and offers discounts, reportedly saving Americans over $20 billion since its inception.

The core services include a website and mobile app that enable real-time searches for the lowest prescription prices at over 70,000 pharmacies across the U.S. Users can access digital coupons and discount cards without needing insurance. GoodRx has also expanded its offerings through telehealth services, allowing for medication refills and virtual consultations, particularly after acquiring HeyDoctor. The company continues to grow, serving 17 million monthly users and focusing on making prescription medications more affordable.

Short Description:
Wellthy is a caregiving support company founded in 2014, dedicated to helping families and employees manage complex care needs throughout all life stages. Operating as a human-led, tech-powered platform, Wellthy connects families with personalized care experts and offers digital tools to streamline the caregiving process. Headquartered in New York City, the company serves millions of sponsored lives across over 190 countries.

Wellthy provides two main product lines: Care Concierge and Backup Care. Care Concierge offers personalized support from a team of over 200 care professionals, assisting with tasks such as sourcing care providers, managing medical records, and coordinating eldercare and childcare. Backup Care addresses urgent care needs for children, loved ones, and pets. The company also features a tech platform that includes task tracking, secure document storage, and a community space for caregivers to connect and share resources. Wellthy serves a diverse client base, including Fortune 500 companies and individual families, and has built a network of over 350,000 vetted care providers.

Carrum Health is a value-based healthcare platform established in 2014. It operates a network of Centers of Excellence, negotiating bundled payments for high-cost specialty care. This approach helps self-funded employers save up to 45% per procedure while providing most enrolled patients with no medical bills. The company focuses on improving outcomes and reducing unnecessary procedures, which can account for a significant portion of employer healthcare spending.

Carrum Health offers all-inclusive bundled payments for surgical and cancer care, covering everything from initial appointments to post-operative therapy. Key services include nationwide access to top-quality providers for conditions like musculoskeletal issues, cancer, and cardiovascular care. The platform also provides patient support through care specialists, ensuring a smooth journey from registration to surgeon selection. Employers benefit from easy implementation, cost savings, and outcome guarantees. Carrum partners with organizations like Quantum Health to enhance service integration and reduce administrative burdens for HR departments.

Infinity Healthcare Consulting is a healthcare consultancy focused on long-term and senior care facilities, primarily in Illinois and Indiana. The company is dedicated to advising nursing homes on improving patient care, operational efficiency, and regulatory compliance. With a team of knowledgeable and compassionate experts, Infinity Healthcare Consulting aims to foster environments of compassion and quality improvement in healthcare centers.

Headquartered in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, the firm offers a range of consulting services tailored to senior care and nursing facilities. These include operational support, clinical guidance, legal advice for regulatory compliance, and customized business development strategies. The goal is to enhance patient care and employee relationships while ensuring facilities meet industry standards. Infinity Healthcare Consulting serves numerous client facilities, including various "Waters of" locations in Indiana and several nursing and rehabilitation homes in Illinois.

Impact Advisors is a healthcare management consulting firm based in Naperville, Illinois, founded in 2007. The company specializes in technology-enabled process improvements aimed at enhancing healthcare delivery, safety, quality, and efficiency. Established by experienced professionals from major U.S. health organizations, Impact Advisors has built a strong reputation as a trusted advisor, earning multiple "Best in KLAS" awards for 17 consecutive years and recognition as a "Best Place to Work" by Modern Healthcare for 14 years.

The firm offers a range of services through two main areas: Strategic Advisory & Operational Improvement Services and Technology Implementation. Their key offerings include business strategy, revenue cycle improvement, clinical process optimization, and digital health solutions. With a commitment to creating a positive impact in healthcare, Impact Advisors serves over 400 clients both nationally and internationally, focusing on employee culture and client value. The current leadership is headed by CEO Todd Hollowell.

HouseWorks is a prominent provider of personalized in-home senior care services, primarily serving the Northeast United States. Founded over 20 years ago in Greater Boston, the company has developed a unique BetterCare at Home™ approach that focuses on quality, technology, and caregiver support. HouseWorks employs over 12,000 caregivers who deliver around 250,000 hours of care weekly, helping older adults remain safe and engaged in their homes.

The company offers a wide range of services tailored to seniors' needs, including home personal care, post-hospital care, respite care, and live-in care. HouseWorks emphasizes caregiver retention through competitive pay, benefits, and ongoing training. They utilize proprietary technology platforms like HouseWorks HUB and eCaring to enhance care coordination and performance insights. With a commitment to quality, HouseWorks serves a diverse clientele, including private-pay older adults and dual-eligible Medicare-Medicaid clients, while partnering with various payors and local organizations.

The company has expanded to multiple locations across Texas, Colorado, Florida, New Mexico, and Arizona, employing around 330 staff members and generating approximately $62 million in revenue.

Aspire offers a variety of services, including personalized allergy treatment plans that help patients build natural tolerance to allergens. Aspire focuses on cost-effective, non-pharmaceutical solutions that require minimal time commitment, having successfully treated over 100,000 patients.

Short Description:
Welldoc is a digital health company based in Columbia, Maryland, founded in 2005 by an endocrinologist. The company focuses on improving chronic disease management, particularly for diabetes, through AI-driven platforms and mobile technology. Welldoc specializes in healthcare technology that integrates personalized, real-time insights into patients' daily lives, enhancing self-management and health outcomes for conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, heart failure, obesity, and behavioral health.

The Welldoc Platform is a scalable digital coaching solution that supports multi-condition management and enables "care anywhere" models for health plans, systems, and employers. Its flagship product, BlueStar®, is an FDA-cleared prescription digital therapeutic for adults with type 2 diabetes, providing in-app coaching and real-time guidance. Welldoc collaborates with various partners, including Samsung Health and LifeScan, to extend care and optimize outcomes for patients. The company has received multiple FDA clearances and has a strong emphasis on evidence-based solutions.

Midwest Orthopaedics at Rush (MOR) is a leading provider of musculoskeletal health services, founded in Chicago in 2003. The practice includes highly regarded orthopedic and spine surgeons who utilize advanced surgical techniques and technology. MOR serves over 85,000 patients each year and has performed more than 19,500 surgical procedures across seven locations in the Chicagoland area. It is consistently ranked as the top orthopedic practice in Illinois and is recognized nationally.

MOR offers a wide range of orthopedic care, including treatment across all specialties, physical therapy, imaging, and bracing/orthotics. The Orthopedic Ambulatory Building (OAB), a 220,000 square-foot facility, serves as a Center of Excellence and features state-of-the-art services such as a Gait Lab and advanced imaging capabilities. MOR also partners with professional sports teams and organizations, including its recent collaboration with Sure Shot Pickleball, providing players with access to injury prevention education and orthopedic care.

Medi-Weightloss® is a physician-supervised medical weight loss and wellness franchise founded in 2005 and headquartered in Tampa, Florida. With over 120 locations across more than 27 states, the company focuses on evidence-based programs that promote sustainable weight loss. Their approach combines individualized care plans developed by physicians, registered dietitians, and health professionals, addressing the common issue of weight regain through medical supervision, education, and ongoing support.

The program features a three-phase approach that includes weekly physician-supervised visits, customized care plans, and prescription medical therapy with FDA-approved medications. Medi-Weightloss also offers vitamin and nutrient injections, IV therapy, and comprehensive medical care for weight-related issues. The company has successfully supported over 435,000 patients, resulting in significant weight loss and health improvements. Medi-Weightloss began franchising in 2008 and continues to prioritize patient satisfaction and accessibility in its services.

It operates eight retirement communities in the Seattle area, focusing on whole body wellness, enriching activities, and person-centered care. RefleXion Medical is a therapeutic oncology company based in Hayward, California, founded in 2009. The company focuses on developing advanced cancer treatment technologies that integrate medical physics, nuclear medicine, and radiotherapy.

The flagship product, the RefleXion® X1 machine with SCINTIX™ biology-guided radiotherapy (BgRT), is the first system to use positron emission tomography (PET) imaging for personalized radiation delivery. This innovative approach allows for real-time tumor tracking during treatment, accommodating moving tumors and enabling the potential treatment of multiple tumors in a single session. The RefleXion X1 has received FDA Breakthrough Device Designation for lung tumors and is applicable for various cancer stages.

RefleXion has successfully raised $450 million in funding from notable investors and employs over 150 people. Todd Powell serves as President and CEO, leading the company in its mission to enhance cancer care through its unique technology.

The Institute for Healthcare Improvement (IHI) is a not-for-profit organization based in Boston, Massachusetts, dedicated to enhancing health and healthcare globally through improvement science. Founded in 1991, IHI mobilizes health systems, communities, and nations to improve outcomes and address challenges such as safety, quality, equity, and population health using evidence-based methods.

IHI serves as a catalyst for change by providing education, training, consulting, and large-scale initiatives. Its IHI Open School has delivered millions of courses, and programs like Transforming Care at the Bedside focus on engaging frontline staff. The organization partners with various health systems to promote quality improvement, patient safety, and equity. IHI's global initiatives include the 100 Million Healthier Lives campaign and the recognition of age-friendly health systems in over 62 countries. Through its work, IHI translates industrial quality improvement methods into healthcare practices, aiming to make care safer, more equitable, and efficient.

Karyopharm Therapeutics Inc. is a commercial-stage pharmaceutical company dedicated to discovering and developing innovative drugs for cancer treatment. The company focuses on targeting nuclear export dysregulation, primarily using its Selective Inhibitor of Nuclear Export (SINE) technology. This approach addresses key mechanisms involved in cancer development, with potential applications extending to neurodegeneration, inflammation, and autoimmune diseases.

Karyopharm's lead product, XPOVIO® (selinexor), is an oral XPO1 inhibitor approved in the U.S. for various hematologic malignancies, including multiple myeloma and relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL). The company also has NEXPOVIO® (selinexor) authorized in Europe for heavily pretreated multiple myeloma patients. Karyopharm is committed to advancing its pipeline, which targets high-unmet-need cancers, and manages the entire drug lifecycle from discovery to commercialization.

Short Description:
The staff of Neosho Memorial serves southeast Kansans with award-winning care.

In 1903, Dr. L.D. Johnson established a private hospital in Chanute, Kan. Forty-four years later, Dr. Johnson decided to close his facility. This decision spurred local mothers groups to start a grassroots campaign to fund the construction of what is known today as Neosho Memorial Regional Medical Center.

On January 14, 1951, Neosho Memorial Hospital opened. The hospital was dedicated as a memorial to the men and women of Neosho County who lost their lives during World War II.

Today, Neosho Memorial continues its history of service to area families. In 2009, the medical center completed a $20 million renovation project creating a state-of-the-art facility with a new emergency department, inpatient unit, intensive care unit, outpatient observation unit and nutrition services department. In addition, the laboratory, imaging services, sleep lab and other areas in the hospital shine with renovated, expanded and improved patient-centered areas.

Governed by a seven-member board appointed by the Neosho County commission, Neosho Memorial is a not-for-profit, county-owned critical access hospital. It is with pride that Neosho Memorial does not require tax dollars for general operations of the medical center.

It is with honor that hospital staff members uphold their commitment to the medical center's mission to promote, improve and restore health by Bringing Excellence and Service Together.

RxSense offers a suite of integrated cloud-based solutions for pharmacy benefits administration. Hippocratic AI collaborates with hospitals, health systems, insurance companies, and telehealth providers across multiple countries, emphasizing partnerships to deliver effective healthcare solutions. Community Medical Centers (CMC) is a not-for-profit network of community health centers located in California's Central Valley, specifically in the Stockton area. GCX Corporation is a prominent manufacturer of medical instrument and IT mounting solutions for the healthcare industry. Founded in 1971 and based in Petaluma, California, the company specializes in the secure and ergonomic positioning of medical devices, patient monitors, and computer hardware. GCX is committed to quality, holding ISO 9001 and ISO 13485 certifications.

Initially established to mount televisions in hospital rooms, GCX has evolved to offer a wide range of healthcare mounting solutions. The company has a strong history of partnerships with major healthcare firms, including Philips Healthcare and GE Healthcare. GCX's product lineup includes wall-mounted systems, mobile solutions like roll stands and carts, and OEM-customized products. Their solutions are designed for hospitals, clinics, and telehealth environments, focusing on mobility, ergonomics, and ease of maintenance. With a workforce of around 305 employees and facilities in California, Texas, and The Netherlands, GCX continues to enhance patient care through innovative mounting solutions.

The health system focuses on improving community health, reducing illness burdens, and enhancing patient experiences through various health initiatives. It serves primarily the residents of Santa Clara County, providing essential public health services and insurance options.

San Francisco Health Plan (SFHP) is dedicated to providing health coverage for San Francisco residents, primarily through Medi-Cal managed care. The plan offers comprehensive no-cost coverage for medical, dental, vision, and related services through a network of doctors, hospitals, and clinics. SFHP aims to be the leading choice for Medi-Cal health coverage, ensuring access to benefits for all California residents, regardless of immigration status.

SFHP's core offerings include the Medi-Cal Plan, which covers a wide range of services such as preventive care, mental health support, and in-home care. The SFHP Care Plus plan is designed for dual-eligible Medicare and Medi-Cal members, combining benefits with additional services like wellness visits and prescription support. The Healthy Workers Plan focuses on outpatient services, requiring doctor referrals for specialty care. All plans emphasize no copayments for most covered services when using in-network providers, making healthcare accessible and affordable for the community.

Lighthouse Guild is a nonprofit organization based in New York City, dedicated to supporting individuals who are blind or visually impaired. Formed in 2014 through the merger of Lighthouse International and the Jewish Guild for the Blind, it has a rich history dating back to the early 1900s. The organization aims to inspire visually impaired individuals to achieve their goals and live independently, offering a range of services that include vision rehabilitation, healthcare, education, and advocacy.

The Guild provides comprehensive services such as adaptive technology training, medical care, behavioral health programs, and educational opportunities, including scholarships for legally blind students. It also focuses on research and innovation in vision healthcare, awarding several prizes for advancements in the field. With a team of healthcare professionals and volunteers, Lighthouse Guild emphasizes health, wellness, and community support to enhance the lives of those it serves.

The company specializes in providing digital health solutions that simplify healthcare for members and employers. MiMedx Group, Inc. is a developer, processor, and marketer of regenerative biomaterial products and bioimplants derived from human placental tissue, skin, and bone. Founded in 2008 and headquartered in Marietta, Georgia, MiMedx has over a decade of experience in providing solutions for chronic and hard-to-heal wounds. The company aims to help the body heal itself through its innovative products, guided by its mission of "Helping Humans Heal."

MiMedx offers a diverse portfolio of products for advanced wound care, burn care, surgical recovery, and more. Key offerings include HydroFix® technology-based medical devices and allografts from human amniotic membrane. These products are utilized across various medical fields, including wound care, surgery, orthopedics, and ophthalmology. MiMedx is recognized as a leading global supplier of amniotic tissue products, having supplied over 700,000 allografts to healthcare professionals worldwide.

Physicians Immediate Care is an urgent care provider that operates as part of WellNow Urgent Care, offering walk-in medical services for non-life-threatening conditions. The facility is designed to provide convenient, high-quality care without the need for appointments, and it accepts most major insurance plans. Physicians Immediate Care has received the Urgent Care Association of America (UCAOA) Accredited Urgent Care designation, a recognition achieved by only about 10% of facilities in the field.

The services offered include treatment for various injuries and illnesses, onsite virtual care, and occupational medicine services such as pre-employment physical exams. Patients can access care from a team of experienced professionals, including board-certified MDs, DOs, physician assistants, and nurse practitioners. The locations support walk-in visits and online check-ins, ensuring accessibility for all patients.

Vitality Group Inc. is the largest behavioral engagement platform in the world, focusing on improving health outcomes and reducing healthcare costs through incentives and behavioral science. With a presence in 41 global markets, the company impacts over 42 million members by promoting healthier behaviors and enhancing lives.

Founded over 30 years ago, Vitality Group originated from South Africa's largest private health plan. The company, formerly known as HumanaVitality, is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. Its core platform offers a behavior-change solution that utilizes clinical science and behavioral design to guide users toward better health. This includes personalized interventions in areas such as physical activity, nutrition, and preventive care. Vitality AI enhances this experience by providing tailored health insights and recommendations.

Vitality Group serves health insurers, health plans, and employers, offering incentivized programs that encourage sustained healthy choices. The platform is recognized for its innovative approach, integrating well-being with care to boost member satisfaction and drive positive health outcomes.

Elation Health is a cloud-based electronic health record (EHR) and practice management platform tailored for independent primary care practices. Founded in 2010 and based in San Francisco, the company serves 24,000 clinicians who care for over 12 million Americans. The platform features an integrated EHR and billing system designed specifically for primary care. Key offerings include a collaborative EHR with a unique three-panel view, an AI-powered medical scribe for automated documentation, and comprehensive billing and practice management tools. Elation also provides specialty-specific workflows, pediatric tools, and extensive interoperability with over 300 integrated partners. The company supports practices of all sizes, from solo clinics to large networks, and emphasizes reducing administrative burdens while enhancing patient care.

Wheel is designed to deliver 24/7 care in all 50 states, utilizing patented smart routing to quickly connect patients with clinicians.

The platform features live video and audio visits, asynchronous messaging, remote lab requisition, integrated e-prescribing, and clinical tools for education and referrals. HealthQuest Physical Therapy (HQPT) is a therapist-owned organization founded in 1999 in Rochester Hills, Michigan. With over 40 locations primarily in Michigan and two other states, HQPT specializes in personalized musculoskeletal rehabilitation for patients of all ages. The company emphasizes patient-centered care, treating individuals with compassion and providing one-on-one attention to improve movement, strength, and quality of life.

HQPT offers a range of services, including orthopedic and neuromuscular rehabilitation, manual therapy, and specialized programs for various conditions such as back and neck pain, sports injuries, and chronic issues like arthritis. The therapists create individualized treatment plans tailored to each patient's medical history, and the organization accepts most insurances while providing free benefits verification. With a commitment to community health education and a focus on fostering staff growth, HQPT aims to be a leading provider in musculoskeletal rehabilitation.

SmithRx operates on a cloud-based platform that features a 100% pass-through pricing model, ensuring that all discounts and manufacturer rebates are directly passed to clients. The platform also offers real-time benefits management and automated claims processing.

The company provides a range of pharmacy benefit management solutions designed for efficiency and savings. These include digital tools for brokers, employers, and members, as well as cost-saving programs that focus on prescription savings and specialty drug management. SmithRx emphasizes transparency and integrity, aiming to secure the lowest cost prescription drugs while improving health outcomes through integrated analytics and clinical support. Its target customers include self-insured employers, brokers, and third-party administrators seeking technology-driven pharmacy solutions.

Jamestown Regional Medical Center in Jamestown, N.D., serves 35,000 people in the heart of rural North Dakota. Esperanza Health Centers is a Federally Qualified Health Center established in 2004 to provide bilingual, high-quality primary care, behavioral health, and wellness services to underserved communities on Chicago's Southwest Side. The organization was founded by local residents and Saint Anthony Hospital administrators to address gaps in accessible health services. It operates five main health sites and serves over 50,000 patients annually, focusing on Latin American immigrant and working-poor families.

The center offers a range of services, including adult and pediatric primary care, women's health, specialized care for teens, behavioral health, and wellness programs. Esperanza also features a PACE program, which provides comprehensive support for seniors, allowing them to age in place with culturally sensitive care. With a commitment to quality and community-rooted services, Esperanza Health Centers emphasizes accessible healthcare for all, regardless of immigration status or ability to pay.

Community Health Group (CHG) is a nonprofit health plan based in San Diego County, established in 1982. It is dedicated to providing accessible and high-quality healthcare primarily to Medi-Cal beneficiaries, serving nearly 400,000 members in the region. As the only San Diego-based not-for-profit managed care plan, CHG focuses on maintaining and improving the health of its members through exceptional service and community partnerships.

CHG specializes in Medi-Cal managed care plans, offering a comprehensive range of medical, behavioral health, and support services for low-income families, children, seniors, and individuals with disabilities. Its key offerings include dual-eligible plans that combine Medicare and Medi-Cal benefits, as well as Medicare plans for those with chronic conditions. The organization emphasizes culturally and linguistically appropriate care, timely claims processing, and local staffing to ensure a personal touch in member interactions. With a commitment to health equity, CHG operates a decentralized network of community providers throughout San Diego County.

With a dedicated team of 25 board-certified orthopedic physicians, 2 interventional pain management providers, and 13 physician assistants, OIP offers comprehensive services that range from prevention to surgery. The organization operates multiple offices across Camp Hill, Harrisburg, Carlisle, Hershey, Millersburg, Sunbury, Newport, and McConnellsburg, employing around 302 staff members.

OIP emphasizes patient-centered care, focusing on alleviating pain, increasing mobility, and improving quality of life. They specialize in treating bone, joint, spine, and sports injuries, with a commitment to prioritizing nonsurgical options whenever possible. Caring Senior Service is a non-medical in-home senior care company founded in 1991 by Jeff Salter. Headquartered in San Antonio, Texas, it operates approximately 50-51 franchise locations across the United States. The company focuses on helping seniors remain healthy, happy, and independent at home through personalized care.

Caring Senior Service offers a range of flexible services tailored to seniors' needs. These include personal care assistance with daily activities, companion care for social interaction, and specialty care for conditions like Alzheimer’s and post-surgery recovery. Additional support services encompass errands, light housekeeping, transportation, and meal preparation. The company utilizes Tendio® software for efficient scheduling and client management, ensuring quality care through its GreatCare® methodology and comprehensive caregiver training.

With a mission to support aging in place, Caring Senior Service serves seniors and their families nationwide, helping them navigate everyday challenges while promoting independence at home.

Short Description:
Viz.ai is a healthcare technology company based in San Francisco, founded in 2016 by Dr. Chris Mansi. The company specializes in an AI-powered care coordination platform that enhances the analysis of medical imaging to detect diseases, speed up diagnoses, and improve workflows in acute care settings. Its platform utilizes over 50 FDA-cleared algorithms to process various imaging modalities, providing real-time insights that facilitate faster triage and treatment decisions for urgent medical conditions.

The core product, Viz.ai One™, is an integrated platform that autodetects suspected diseases across multiple therapeutic areas. It includes specialized suites such as the Viz Neuro Suite for neurovascular diseases, the Viz Vascular Suite for vascular conditions, the Viz Cardio Suite for cardiac imaging, the Viz Trauma Suite for trauma assessment, and the Viz Radiology Suite for optimizing radiologist workflows. The company has expanded its offerings to cover 12 FDA-cleared disease areas and has received recognition for its innovative impact on patient outcomes, being deployed in over 1,700 hospitals and health systems across the U.S. and Europe.

Short Description:
Virta Health is a digital health company dedicated to reversing type 2 diabetes and obesity through personalized nutrition programs and virtual medical care. Founded in 2014 by Sami Inkinen, Virta operates as an online specialty medical clinic that integrates nutritional biochemistry, data science, and continuous support from healthcare providers to help patients achieve sustainable weight loss and diabetes reversal.

The company serves over 12 million lives in the U.S. and partners with approximately 550 employers, health plans, and government entities. Virta's comprehensive care model includes diabetes reversal programs, sustainable weight loss support, and ongoing diabetes management, all centered on personalized nutrition with dietary carbohydrate restriction. Their approach is backed by extensive clinical research, demonstrating significant improvements in health markers and successful outcomes for patients. Virta's business model emphasizes performance-based guarantees, ensuring measurable savings and improved health outcomes for its clients.

Short Description:
Kyruus Health is a healthcare technology company founded by physicians, focused on enhancing access to care through its leading care access platform. The platform connects patients with the right healthcare providers by uniting comprehensive provider data, which helps streamline patient journeys from search to payment. Following its acquisition by RevSpring, Kyruus Health serves a wide range of clients, including health systems, hospitals, medical groups, and health plans across the U.S., connecting over 425,000 providers and 150 million health plan members.

The Kyruus Connect platform is designed to improve healthcare navigation and transparency. It features capabilities such as provider data management, patient engagement tools, digital intake, and scheduling. The platform integrates with electronic health records and digital health networks, enabling stakeholders to make informed decisions and automate workflows. By optimizing access and operations, Kyruus Health aims to create a more efficient healthcare system that benefits everyone involved.

Short Description:
Vital Care Infusion Services is a franchisor of infusion pharmacies, established in 1986 as the first franchise model for infusion services. Headquartered in Brentwood, Tennessee, it ranks as the fifth largest infusion provider in the U.S. and is recognized as the fastest-growing pharmacy franchise in this sector. The company is owned by Berkshire Partners, a private equity firm.

With a network of over 160 infusion pharmacy locations across approximately two dozen states, Vital Care serves secondary, tertiary, and underserved communities nationwide. The company offers safe and convenient infusion therapy services for patients with both acute and chronic conditions, delivered in the comfort of their homes or other settings. Vital Care's mission is to enhance access to high-quality infusion therapy and improve patient outcomes through professional services provided by locally owned pharmacies. As a franchisor, it supports its franchisees with a comprehensive business model that includes proprietary processes and dedicated support, allowing them to focus on patient care.

Three Oaks Hospice is a hospice and palliative care provider based in Dallas, Texas, founded in May 2019 by David Gasmire and Andrea Bohannon. The company focuses on Medicare patients and operates in multiple states, including Texas, Missouri, Kansas, and Illinois. With a mission centered on listening, caring, and serving, Three Oaks emphasizes compassionate care for individuals with life-limiting illnesses.

The organization offers a range of services, including pain and symptom management, palliative care, family support, and specialized care programs for conditions like ALS and Alzheimer's disease. Their Tru360™ program enhances patient-focused care and has shown to reduce medication costs significantly. Three Oaks Hospice has expanded through acquisitions and serves a diverse clientele, working closely with patients, families, and healthcare providers to create personalized care plans.

Imaging Healthcare Specialists (IHS) is a San Diego-based provider of outpatient diagnostic imaging services, operating nine centers across the region, including locations in Oceanside, Vista, Encinitas, Poway, La Jolla, Chula Vista, and San Diego. Founded in 2005, IHS employs approximately 317 people and generates around $80.2 million in revenue. The company is known for its commitment to advanced technology, specialized expertise, and exceptional patient service.

IHS offers a comprehensive range of diagnostic imaging services, including CT scans, interventional radiology, mammography, MRI, ultrasound, and X-ray. They also provide specialized screenings such as CT calcium scoring and low-dose lung screening. The company emphasizes patient-focused care, ensuring respect for time, privacy, safety, and budget. IHS features a user-friendly online portal for scheduling and appointment management, enhancing accessibility for patients and healthcare providers.
