HealthTrackRx is a leading PCR-based infectious disease laboratory based in Denton, Texas. With over 20 years of experience, the company specializes in rapid molecular diagnostic testing for healthcare providers across the nation. HealthTrackRx is a fully licensed and accredited clinical laboratory, employing approximately 300-419 people and generating around $44 million in annual revenue. The company holds a BBB Accreditation with an A+ rating.

The core services offered by HealthTrackRx include next-morning PCR testing results, pathogen detection and identification, antimicrobial stewardship programs, and substance use disorder testing. The company has pioneered rapid PCR testing and has partnered with UPS Healthcare® to enhance the delivery of test results. HealthTrackRx meets all state and federal requirements for clinical laboratory testing and has received FDA Emergency Use Authorization for its COVID-19 tests. It serves tens of thousands of healthcare providers, contributing to improved health outcomes and patient care.

Rightway Healthcare is a healthcare technology company based in New York City, founded in 2017. It specializes in pharmacy benefit management (PBM) and clinical care navigation, combining human expertise with technology to help members access high-value medications and care. The company focuses on optimizing employer healthcare costs through transparent pricing and clinician-led support.

Headquartered at 55 Hudson Yards, Rightway employs around 200 people and has experienced significant growth, including $130 million in total funding and $42 million in revenue. The company serves hundreds of thousands of Fortune 500 employees and millions of members nationwide, emphasizing aligned incentives and transparency. Rightway offers a fully integrated PBM and care navigation platform that includes personalized clinical support, an intuitive mobile and web app, and tailored implementation services. Its innovative approach has led to notable medical cost savings and high client retention rates.

WorkCare, Inc. is a physician-led occupational health, wellness, and absence management company based in Anaheim, California. Founded in 1984, it has over 40 years of experience in providing proactive solutions to protect employee health from hire to retire. The company serves a diverse range of businesses, from small enterprises to global corporations, managing over 150,000 injuries annually through its 24/7 triage program and employing around 700 staff.

WorkCare offers a comprehensive suite of occupational health solutions designed to enhance workforce safety and productivity. Their services include occupational health screenings, incident prevention programs, injury care and management, on-site clinical services, wellness solutions, and absence and leave management. The company also provides regulatory compliance support and additional offerings like direct physician consultations and event medical services. With a focus on preventive care and rapid response, WorkCare helps employers meet health requirements while improving recruitment and employee morale.

PillPack is an online pharmacy founded in 2013 by TJ Parker and Elliot Cohen. The company simplifies medication management by pre-sorting prescriptions into personalized daily packets and delivering them directly to customers' homes. It focuses on helping the 30 million U.S. adults who take five or more medications daily. Acquired by Amazon in 2018, PillPack operates as an independent subsidiary, aiming to improve traditional pharmacy inefficiencies.

PillPack's main service is a mail-order pharmacy that organizes medications into labeled, single-dose packets with time-of-day instructions. These packets are shipped every two weeks, allowing for easy management of maintenance medications. The service is supported by an online platform that offers 24/7 assistance and automatic refills. The company has grown significantly since its inception, distributing over a million dose packets and securing licenses in 47 states.

Wilmington Health is a physician-owned, multi-specialty healthcare practice based in Wilmington, North Carolina. Established in 1971, it serves over half a million residents in the Wilmington area and nearby Jacksonville, operating across three states. As the second-largest Accountable Care Organization (ACO) in the country by reach, Wilmington Health focuses on delivering high-quality care through its primary care providers and specialists.

The practice offers a wide range of services, including family medicine, cardiology, urgent care, and occupational medicine. Its Today’s Care and Today’s Care+ centers provide walk-in services for minor ailments, while WH Direct offers employer-focused health services. Wilmington Health is also involved in clinical research, conducting over 50 trials to support new treatments. Additionally, it provides consulting services through its Management Services Organization (MSO) to other ACOs, enhancing operational expertise in value-based care. The organization is committed to improving patient experience and advancing provider work-life balance.

Founded in 1974, the company employs approximately 392-404 staff and generates annual revenue of $23.2 million. SuperCare Health partners with healthcare professionals, clinicians, and insurance providers to deliver in-home patient management solutions that aim to reduce hospital readmissions and support patient independence.

The company offers a wide range of therapies and programs focused on chronic respiratory diseases, including COPD, cystic fibrosis, and sleep apnea. Their services include ventilation programs, oxygen therapy, sleep apnea solutions, and medication optimization. SuperCare Health also provides diabetes care and pediatric solutions through their SuperKids™ program. They supply durable medical equipment such as ventilators, CPAP/BiPAP devices, and mobility aids, ensuring high-quality care through innovative technologies and personalized support.

North Bend Medical Center (NBMC) is a prominent multi-specialty outpatient clinic located in Coos Bay, Oregon, serving the Southern Oregon coastline. With over 400 employees, including around 100 healthcare providers, NBMC is dedicated to delivering professional and compassionate care. The center operates from its main facility and has satellite clinics in Bandon, Coquille, and Myrtle Point, ensuring accessible healthcare for local communities.

NBMC offers a wide range of medical services, including primary and specialty care, surgical procedures, diagnostic imaging, and therapy and rehabilitation. Key services encompass family medicine, pediatrics, women's health, and various surgical specialties. The Immediate Care Clinic provides convenient care for non-life-threatening issues, while specialized clinics address needs such as anticoagulation management and behavioral health. With modern facilities and a commitment to quality care, NBMC is positioned as the largest multi-specialty clinic in the region, focused on serving the healthcare needs of South Coast residents.

eHealth Technologies is a healthcare technology company founded in 2006 and based in West Henrietta, New York. The company specializes in solutions that streamline the collection, organization, and delivery of medical records, images, and test results. By providing fast digital access to comprehensive patient medical histories, eHealth Technologies helps clinicians make informed decisions quickly, improving treatment times for patients in various care scenarios, including cancer treatment and emergency services.

The company offers an advanced medical records retrieval platform that consolidates patient data from multiple sources into a user-friendly digital format. This platform reduces administrative burdens for healthcare systems and enhances patient throughput. eHealth Technologies serves over 100 hospital systems across the United States, including a significant portion of the top-ranked hospitals. The company has been recognized for its rapid growth and commitment to improving patient outcomes through innovative technology solutions.

CHMB, or California Healthcare Medical Billing, is a California-based company founded in 1995. It specializes in technology and business services aimed at optimizing healthcare performance, profitability, and revenue cycle management. CleanSlate Centers is a national medical group based in Brentwood, Tennessee, founded in 2009. The company operates over 65 physician-led outpatient addiction treatment centers that focus on evidence-based care for opioid and alcohol use disorders, along with related mental and behavioral health conditions. CleanSlate emphasizes high-quality, integrated care, combining medication-assisted treatment (MAT) with behavioral health therapies and social support services.

The company specializes in anatomic and clinical pathology services tailored for the podiatric medical community. BakoDx focuses on diagnostics related to nail, skin, wound, soft tissue, and bone conditions of the lower extremity. With a dedicated team of 264 employees, BakoDx generates approximately $28.1 million in revenue and is recognized for its commitment to diagnostic excellence and education.

BakoDx offers a wide range of services, including proprietary PCR tests, therapeutic solutions for skin and nail conditions, and biopsy instruments. BakoDx's in-network services are accessible to over 250 million lives across major national health plans and numerous insurers, ensuring patient-friendly billing practices. Mee Memorial Healthcare System (MMHS) is a nonprofit rural healthcare provider based in King City, California. It serves Southern Monterey County as the only hospital within a 50-mile radius, delivering culturally sensitive, patient-centered care through its hospital and network of clinics. Established in 1941, MMHS has evolved from a small facility into a modern hospital with 94 licensed beds, emphasizing high-quality service and health equity.

MMHS offers a range of inpatient and outpatient services, including primary healthcare, diagnostic testing, and specialized outpatient services. The organization operates four medical clinics in King City and one in Greenfield, all functioning as Rural Health Clinics. With a focus on community support and partnerships, MMHS addresses healthcare challenges while promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion. The organization has received recognition for its contributions to the community, including being named the 2021 Business of the Year by the King City Chamber of Commerce.

Shiel Medical Laboratory is one of the oldest continuously operating clinical laboratories in the United States, known for its expertise in clinical and anatomic pathology testing. Based in the Brooklyn Navy Yard, it was the largest privately held lab in New York City, serving the New York-New Jersey metropolitan area. The company has adapted to changes in healthcare, including electronic health record integration and molecular testing, employing over 600 people at its peak.

Shiel offered a comprehensive range of clinical and anatomic pathology tests, focusing on high-quality laboratory services to support healthcare providers. Its clientele included private physicians, group practices, union and industrial accounts, long-term care and home care agencies, and drug rehabilitation centers. In December 2017, Shiel was acquired by Quest Diagnostics, while Fresenius Medical Care retained its separate dialysis-related lab services.

Centre for Neuro Skills (CNS) is a healthcare services company based in Bakersfield, California, founded in 1980. It specializes in post-acute rehabilitation programs for individuals recovering from traumatic and acquired brain injuries, strokes, and concussions. CNS operates multiple locations across California and Texas, employing around 612 staff and generating approximately $73.5 million in revenue.

CNS focuses on a patient-centered approach that emphasizes dignity and respect. Its mission is to reduce disability and maximize independence for brain injury patients through tailored rehabilitation programs. These programs include intensive modified inpatient, outpatient, residential, and assisted living services, all designed to promote skill generalization and community reintegration. The company also provides informational resources, such as newsletters and a quarterly magazine, to keep families and patients informed about advancements in brain injury care. CNS is recognized for its outcome-driven, cost-effective programs that aim to improve the quality of life for its patients.

It operates as part of Beth Israel Lahey Health, which includes a network of academic medical centers and community hospitals.

BID Needham offers a range of services across multiple specialties, including emergency medicine, heart care, cancer care, and orthopedics. The hospital emphasizes high-quality, accessible care and collaborates closely with Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center to provide advanced medical services locally. It is committed to community benefit initiatives, focusing on serving seniors and school populations, and has received numerous awards for excellence in patient care.

The START Center for Cancer Care, based in San Antonio, specializes in advanced cancer treatment and early-phase clinical trials. With over 40 years of experience, the center combines personalized care with innovative research to enhance patient quality of life and contribute to cancer cures. It offers a range of services, including surgery, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, immunotherapy, and genetic testing, all delivered through multiple locations in South Texas.

The START Center for Cancer Research operates as a significant community-based oncology research network, focusing on early-phase trials. It has contributed to the approval of over 45 therapies and is expanding its capabilities in hematological cancer trials. The center emphasizes community access to innovative treatment options, working closely with patients, families, and healthcare providers to create tailored treatment plans. Its programs include specialized care for breast, upper GI, and skin cancers, along with supportive services like oncology rehabilitation and wellness programs.

Health Management Associates (HMA) is an independent national research and consulting firm based in Lansing, Michigan, specializing in healthcare and human services. Founded in 1985, HMA has grown to over 500 employees through mergers and acquisitions, forming the HMA Family of Companies. The firm brings together expertise from clinicians, policy leaders, analysts, and actuaries to tackle complex challenges in policy, business, and operations.

HMA offers a wide range of services, including strategic guidance, technical assistance, data analysis, policy development, and capacity building for nonprofits. The firm emphasizes the importance of local healthcare dynamics and integrates various aspects of health and social services to improve outcomes and reduce costs. HMA also provides analytical models and resources to support critical decision-making, along with insights through podcasts and expert commentary. Its diverse clientele includes policymakers, health plans, community organizations, and government agencies, addressing national and regional healthcare issues.

Established in 1954, it serves Clarion County and surrounding areas as a nonprofit community-teaching facility. The hospital offers a range of services, including a 24/7 emergency department, inpatient care, surgical services, and a cancer center.

The hospital features advanced diagnostic imaging capabilities and provides various outpatient services such as wound care, pain management, and rehabilitation. Specialized care is available for psychiatric services, stroke care, and sleep medicine. Sun Life Health is a not-for-profit organization and the largest provider of primary health care services in Pinal County, Arizona. It serves over 47,000 patients, including 28% children, across various communities such as Apache Junction, Casa Grande, and Chandler. The organization is governed by a Board of Directors that represents patients and communities, focusing on patient well-being and quality of care.

Sun Life Health delivers services through the Primary Care Medical Home (PCMH) model, which fosters collaboration between patients, physicians, and families. It offers primary medical care for families, including same-day TeleHealth appointments. Additionally, it provides Ryan White Part A services for HIV-positive residents in Pinal County, ensuring access to regular medical care. The organization conducts community health needs assessments every three years to better understand and address local health issues.

Opened in 2012, it operates as a joint venture between Ascension and Ardent Health Services. It is part of Ascension's network in Central Texas, which includes several other healthcare facilities.

SMCHH offers a wide range of medical services, including emergency care, cardiology, general and orthopedic surgery, gastroenterology, imaging services, and physical therapy. SMCHH is accredited by the Joint Commission and recognized as an Accredited Chest Pain Center and an Advanced Primary Stroke Center, ensuring high standards in patient safety and care quality. Its mission is to serve the community with exceptional healthcare, reflecting its commitment to the well-being of the local population.

Founded in 2007 by David Jackson, the company focuses on delivering compassionate care to enhance the lives of geriatric patients and their families. Its mission is to provide excellent healthcare through a full continuum of services, guided by the Pillars of Choice.

The company offers a wide range of services, including home health and hospice care, rehabilitation therapies, and personal care. These services encompass skilled nursing, physical, speech, and occupational therapies, as well as professional caregiving and care management. Partners Health Management is a public Local Management Entity/Managed Care Organization based in Gastonia, North Carolina. Founded in 2012, it manages Medicaid, state, and local funds to provide integrated behavioral and physical health services across 15 counties in North Carolina. The organization serves over one million residents, focusing on mental health, intellectual and developmental disabilities, substance use disorders, and traumatic brain injuries.

As a Member Care Organization, Partners Health emphasizes whole-person, community-centered care by collaborating with local providers and stakeholders. It oversees the quality of care provided by contracted providers, ensuring access to necessary services without delivering direct care itself. Key initiatives include diabetes prevention and management, asthma management for youth, and various quality improvement programs. With an annual revenue of approximately $46.3 million, Partners Health is committed to enhancing health outcomes for its members through effective resource management and support.

Charles E. Smith Life Communities (CESLC) is a nonprofit organization with over 115 years of experience in providing senior living and care services. Located on a 38-acre campus in Rockville, Maryland, CESLC is dedicated to honoring older adults through a full continuum of care rooted in Jewish values. The organization began in 1910 as a small initiative to support aging Jewish community members and has grown significantly over the years, officially becoming CESLC in 2006.

CESLC offers a range of living options, including independent living apartments, assisted living, long-term care, and skilled nursing services. The campus features multiple facilities, such as the Wasserman Residence and the Hirsh Health Center, which provides outpatient medical care. Additionally, CESLC offers homecare services and specialized programs like the ElderSAFE™ Center, which supports elder abuse victims. With a commitment to fostering meaningful connections and promoting successful aging, CESLC serves over 1,100 older adults in the Greater Washington, D.C. area.

As a critical access hospital, Cass Regional Medical Center maintains a 21-bed medical/surgical unit, a four-bed intensive care unit, and a 10-bed behavioral health unit. Services provided by Cass Regional Medical Center include emergency care; general and specialty surgery; and rehabilitation services which include nationally-recognized cardiac rehabilitation and diabetes education programs. The hospital offers advanced diagnostic capabilities such as MRI, CT and PET-CT scanning, digital and 3-D mammography, nuclear medicine and a nationally-accredited sleep study lab.

Specialists in nearly 20 different areas treat patients on the Cass Regional campus in either the medical center or the adjoining Rock Haven Medical Mall, which is home to Cass Regional's Rock Haven Specialty Clinic, Cass Regional Orthopedics, and Harrisonville Medical Clinic (family medicine, general surgery, and ear, nose and throat). The Wound Center at Rock Haven Specialty Clinic provides comprehensive treatment for chronic, slow-to-heal and serious wounds. Mizell Memorial Hospital first opened its door almost 70 years ago. From a 29-bed facility in 1949, the hospital added a wing in 1957, followed by a new surgical wing in 1963. Changes throughout the years have included addition of in-house nuclear medical services, Certified Sleep Center, Senior Behavioral Care Unit, and various renovation projects of different areas. In addition to the hospital facility, Mizell also expanded services to include a world class Wellness Center and the addition of the Mizell Medical Clinic for primary care. The active medical staff consists of physicians and surgeons in family practice, pediatrics, radiology, gynecology, general surgery and orthopedic surgery. Mizell Memorial Hospital employees over 200 hundred people that are committed to the hospital's mission of providing quality healthcare with hometown compassion. 

"Mizell Memorial Hospital is a private, not-for-profit, acute care facility, governed by a local volunteer board of directors made up of individuals from the community," said Jana Wyatt, hospital CEO. This type of local leadership is what allows the hospital to grow with the needs of the community. 
"We want to provide first-class services, as good as any facility in the nation. We want the care we give here to be as good or better than you get anywhere else. How we achieve this is to have world-class employees and physicians," Jana Wyatt, CEO said. 

Mizell Memorial Hospital strives every day for our family to take care of your families and to everyday make a difference.

DeliverHealth is a healthcare technology and services company based in Madison, Wisconsin, with additional offices in Clearwater, Florida. Founded in 2021, it emerged from the carve-out of Nuance Communications' health information management and electronic health record services. The company focuses on simplifying EHR documentation, revenue cycle management, and clinical workflows for hospitals and health systems. By leveraging AI and natural language processing, DeliverHealth aims to reduce clinician burdens and enhance patient outcomes.

DeliverHealth serves over 800 health systems and 60,000 providers across the United States, Canada, and New Zealand, with a customer base of approximately 2,000. The company offers a suite of software-as-a-service solutions, including AI-driven transcription systems, intelligent automation for revenue cycle management, and digital health tools for patient engagement. Its mission emphasizes a "patient-first" approach, prioritizing technology solutions that streamline documentation and improve the overall healthcare experience. With a team of about 2,500 employees, DeliverHealth continues to innovate and expand its offerings in the healthcare sector.

Valley Regional Hospital (VRH), also known as Valley Regional Healthcare, is a community-focused acute-care hospital located in Claremont, New Hampshire. Serving the Greater Claremont area since 1893, VRH has transformed from its origins as Cottage Hospital into a modern healthcare center that emphasizes preventative and acute care services. The hospital partners with Dartmouth Health to provide access to specialists for complex medical needs, including cancer care.

VRH offers a wide range of medical services, including emergency care, preventative care, and advanced imaging technology. The hospital is dedicated to supporting its patients through the Community Health Team, which provides free assistance for primary care patients, addressing social needs such as housing and food insecurity. VRH is committed to accessibility, offering care regardless of insurance status or ability to pay, with financial assistance programs available for eligible uninsured or under-insured patients.

Nurses Unlimited, Inc. is a for-profit home health care provider based in Odessa, Texas, established in 1983. The company specializes in compassionate in-home services, including skilled nursing, personal care, medication management, physical therapy, attendant care, pediatric nursing, and respite care. Following its acquisition by AccentCare, Inc. in 2017, Nurses Unlimited expanded its reach across Texas while maintaining its brand and local leadership.

Nurses Unlimited employs between 100 and 249 people and serves over 3,600 patients, primarily in west Texas. The organization emphasizes patient independence, family involvement, and open communication, providing holistic support tailored to the needs of individuals with chronic illnesses, post-operative recovery, and pediatric care.

Hummingbird Healthcare is a tech-enabled managed services company based in Miami, Florida. The company focuses on enhancing patient access to healthcare through optimized operations and technology integration, particularly with Epic systems. With a small team of fewer than 25 employees, Hummingbird aims to reduce healthcare costs associated with phone calls by providing personalized patient experiences similar to those in consumer industries.

The company offers two main service lines: Patient Access as a Service and Epic as a Service. Patient Access as a Service includes appointment scheduling, insurance verification, nurse triage, and automated prescription management. Epic as a Service focuses on optimizing Epic systems through issue evaluation, training programs, and analytics support. Hummingbird also emphasizes a people-first model, aiming to upskill employees and align technology with patient care needs. 

Hummingbird has successfully raised $30 million in funding to support its growth and technology scaling efforts, partnering with organizations like UCHealth to modernize patient access for health systems.

Short Description:
Weiss Memorial Hospital was a 236-bed urban hospital located in the Uptown neighborhood of Chicago's North Side. Founded in 1953 through the vision of Louis A. Weiss, the hospital aimed to serve the diverse communities of Chicago's northside. It was the first nonprofit community hospital built in Chicago in 25 years and provided a full range of medical and surgical specialties, along with comprehensive emergency services.

The hospital was dedicated to offering quality, individualized, and compassionate care. At its peak, it employed over 450 doctors across 40 medical specialties and more than 800 staff members. Weiss Memorial Hospital was known for its community focus, originally established to support Jewish and immigrant populations and to provide care to those excluded from other hospitals. It included outreach programs and support groups to further assist the community. 

In 2022, Weiss Memorial Hospital was acquired by Resilience Healthcare. The hospital ceased most operations on August 8, 2025, but continues to offer select outpatient services.

Kauffman Fellows is a nonprofit organization that runs the Kauffman Fellows Program, a two-year initiative designed for future venture capitalists. Established in 1995 by the Ewing Marion Kauffman Foundation, the program focuses on education, networking, and leadership development to cultivate innovation leaders skilled in capital formation. Since its inception, it has trained over 360 fellows from diverse backgrounds across 50 countries, promoting entrepreneurship and collaboration within the innovation ecosystem.

The program offers rigorous education and training in venture capital best practices, along with opportunities for networking and mentorship. It fosters trust-based relationships among fellows, enabling collaborative investments in various startups. Kauffman Fellows also disseminates knowledge through publications like the Kauffman Fellows Report and other journals, sharing insights on innovation and capital formation. The organization emphasizes leadership development, preparing fellows for impactful roles in venture capital and beyond, with many continuing in the field for an average of 15 years after completing the fellowship.

Short Description:
American Physician Partners (APP) was a physician-led medical management company that specialized in emergency medicine and hospital medicine services. Founded in 2015 and based in Brentwood, Tennessee, APP focused on delivering quality improvements and practice stability at partner hospitals. The company employed over 2,500 physicians and advanced practice clinicians, providing care to more than 4 million patients annually across over 170 practice sites.

APP's operational model included Site Medical Directors at each practice to lead provider teams and maintain strong relationships with hospitals. The company also offered comprehensive back-office support, allowing clinicians to concentrate on patient care. APP partnered with various hospitals, including Borgess Medical Center in Michigan and Mercy Health Lourdes Hospital in Kentucky, to enhance healthcare delivery. Despite its rapid growth, APP ceased operations in July 2023 due to financial pressures.

Tang+Company is a family-owned provider of integrated occupational health, safety, and compliance solutions, with over 40 years of experience. Based in Long Beach, California, the company employs nearly 400 experts who serve businesses nationwide. Tang+Company specializes in environmental, health, and safety (EH&S) management, focusing on improving compliance and reducing safety incidents for large and medium-sized companies.

The company offers a range of services through three core lines: CAREonsite for occupational healthcare, ASAPcheck for employee screening, and OSCAsafe for safety training. Their services include on-site medical staffing, telemedicine, drug and alcohol testing, background checks, and personalized safety training. Tang+Company also provides advanced data and tech solutions, including their proprietary Electronic Command Center (ECC™) for managing electronic medical records and reporting. They cater to various industries, particularly high-risk sectors like refineries, and have a strong presence with over 2,400 locations across the country.

Short Description:
Rehab Without Walls® Neuro Rehabilitation (RWW) is a specialized provider of neuro rehabilitation services with over 30 years of experience. Owned by BrightSpring Health Services, RWW focuses on delivering evidence-based care for individuals with complex neurological conditions. The company operates in home, community, and outpatient settings, blending scientific methods with creative approaches to achieve superior rehabilitation outcomes.

RWW offers a comprehensive, interdisciplinary approach to treating conditions such as acquired brain injuries, traumatic brain injuries, spinal cord injuries, stroke recovery, and advanced neurological conditions. Their services include individualized rehabilitation in patients' homes, day treatment programs, outpatient clinics, and residential facilities for intensive care. RWW employs various specialized therapies, including occupational therapy and neuropsychological assessments, to address the diverse needs of their patients.

The organization is dedicated to helping both adults and children maintain independence and improve their quality of life. RWW accepts multiple insurance types and publishes annual impact reports to highlight their outcomes and contributions to the community.

Short Description:
CNY Fertility is a fertility treatment provider established in 1997, headquartered in Syracuse, New York. The organization focuses on delivering comprehensive, accessible, and affordable reproductive healthcare solutions. With a revenue of $8.4 million and a team of 318 employees, CNY Fertility has successfully supported the birth of over 30,000 babies. Their motto, "Making Priceless Affordable®," reflects their commitment to offering high-quality fertility treatments at about one-third of the typical cost.

CNY Fertility provides a wide range of reproductive services, including advanced procedures like egg retrievals and embryo transfers, as well as diagnostic and ancillary services. They offer introductory treatments such as ovulation induction and intrauterine insemination (IUI), along with advanced options like in vitro fertilization (IVF) and egg freezing. The company also emphasizes integrated wellness services and conducts research through the CNY Fertility Research Institute. With multiple locations across the U.S. and Canada, CNY Fertility ensures that individuals and couples can access fertility care, including remote coordination with healthcare providers.

Short Description:
Saint Francis Hospital-Bartlett is a full-service healthcare facility located in the Memphis region, established in 2004. It is part of the Saint Francis Health System, which began in 1960 in Tulsa, Oklahoma. The Bartlett location was developed to meet the healthcare needs of the growing population in the area.

The hospital offers a range of acute care services, including cardiovascular care with catheterization labs, an emergency department with 33 beds, and surgical services featuring eight operating rooms. It also provides obstetric services for maternal and newborn care, along with general acute care across various specialties. In 2012, the hospital underwent a significant expansion, adding 83,000 square feet and enhancing its service capabilities.

Saint Francis Hospital-Bartlett serves the Memphis metropolitan area, including Arlington, where it has physician offices to address local healthcare needs. The hospital is dedicated to quality improvement and community care, aiming to be recognized as a "community built on care."

Short Description:
RQI Partners, LLC is a collaboration between the American Heart Association and Laerdal Medical, established in 2018. The company focuses on enhancing survival rates from sudden cardiac arrest through modernized CPR training and resuscitation education programs. Their mission emphasizes the importance of CPR competence and confidence over mere course completion.

The company offers a range of resuscitation training solutions, including the Resuscitation Quality Improvement (RQI) Program, which utilizes data-driven, evidence-based methods for CPR training. They also provide HeartCode eLearning Programs for various life support courses and simulation-based training that allows healthcare professionals to practice skills flexibly. RQI 2020 is another initiative aimed at improving CPR quality while reducing costs for healthcare systems.

RQI Partners serves a wide array of customers, including over 2,400 acute care hospitals and health systems, EMS agencies, and nursing programs across U.S. colleges and universities. The company aims to save one million additional lives annually by 2030, with a goal of saving 50,000 lives from preventable cardiac arrest each year by 2025.

CWC offers a wide range of comprehensive women's healthcare services, including general obstetric and gynecologic care, high-risk pregnancy management, postpartum care, and various surgical procedures. The company specializes in infusion therapy for complex, chronic, and rare conditions, prioritizing patient-first care through compassionate teams that coordinate closely with patients, families, and healthcare providers.

Short Description:
Neighborhood Health Center (NHC) is a Federally Qualified Health Center serving the Western New York region, including Buffalo, Blasdell, and Hamburg. NHC provides ambulatory healthcare and wellness services to all individuals, focusing on those who are medically under-insured, uninsured, and homeless. The organization emphasizes kindness, resilience, teamwork, and fairness in delivering high-quality, patient-centered primary healthcare.

NHC offers a wide range of services under one roof, including primary care, women's health, dental services, and specialty services such as podiatry and nutritional counseling. They also provide diagnostic testing, pharmacy services, and treatment for substance use disorders. Additional support includes community health initiatives, healthcare for the homeless, and assistance with health insurance information. NHC accepts most insurances and offers sliding fee scale discounts for uninsured patients, along with transportation assistance to help clients reach their appointments.

Stratus is a leading provider of neurodiagnostic testing solutions in the U.S., specializing in EEG technologies and services. Headquartered in Irving, Texas, Stratus focuses on expediting patient diagnoses for epilepsy and seizure-like disorders. The company offers a comprehensive range of services, including in-home video EEG, continuous EEG monitoring, and home sleep studies, all supported by cloud-based technology for easy access and review by physicians.

Founded in 2020, Stratus has roots in sleep testing and has since served over 120,000 patients and thousands of physicians nationwide. The company is recognized for its innovative approach, leveraging a large database of EEG data for research and clinical applications. StratusEEG™ software facilitates efficient EEG workflow, while its hardware solutions enhance diagnostic accuracy. Stratus is committed to improving clinical outcomes and providing cost-effective, home-based services to the neurology community.

Short Description:
Campbell Clinic Orthopaedics, founded in 1909 by Dr. Willis C. Campbell in Memphis, Tennessee, is a prominent orthopaedic medical practice known for its comprehensive care, education, and research. The clinic has a rich history, having expanded from a single practice to a full-service hospital and outpatient facility. It was the first to establish an orthopaedic residency program in 1924 and continues to partner with the University of Tennessee Health Science Center to train future surgeons.

The clinic offers specialized orthopaedic surgery and treatment services, including fracture management and care across various subspecialties. It employs over 90 providers and more than 800 staff, ensuring high standards of patient care. A notable contribution to the field is *Campbell's Operative Orthopaedics*, a widely recognized textbook that serves as a key reference in orthopaedic education globally. The clinic also emphasizes education through the Campbell Clinic Foundation, which oversees its residency program and supports national and international lecturing by its physicians.

Short Description:
North Valley Hospital is a 25-bed critical access hospital located in beautiful Whitefish, Montana. The hospital opened its current facility in 2007 on a 45 acre campus and serves a population of 30,000 people in the Flathead Valley area. North Valley Hospital employs 450 people in the area and has approximately 160 active, courtesy, and consulting physicians on staff in addition to a community trauma center and 24/7 emergency, lab, and imaging departments. The hospital was the first in the area to acquire a DaVinci Robot for surgery. Also, in 2015, our spa-like birth center welcomed 541 newborns. 

North Valley Hospital is a Planetree certified facility, and the hospital and all outreach clinics practice the Planetree healthcare philosophy of patient centered care in a healing environment.


Our Mission, Vision, and Values

Our Mission is to be the center of healing for the communities we serve.

Our Vision is to collectively serve as a catalyst for healing of the mind, body, and spirit in a patient-centered environment, and to commit to stewardship of healthcare resources in order to continually improve the health of the communities we serve.

We value:

Patients and their families as partners in healthcare who require our professionalism, confidentiality and attention to individual needs. 

Caring behaviors that exhibit integrity, respect, and commitment to creating a life-enhancing and healing environment.

Teamwork with open communication and participation dedicated to furthering a shared vision.

Leadership from our hospital and foundation boards, medical and hospital staff, patients and communities that exemplifies flexibility, innovation, and support.

Evergreen Health is a community-based healthcare organization established in 1983, originally as AIDS Community Services. It has grown to provide a wide range of medical, supportive, and behavioral health services to underserved communities in Western New York. With over 40 years of experience, Evergreen Health is dedicated to fostering healthy communities, particularly for individuals living with chronic illnesses.

The organization offers comprehensive services, including HIV/AIDS care, primary and specialty medical care, pharmacy services, and behavioral health treatment. It also provides harm reduction services, such as syringe exchange programs and rapid testing for HIV and HCV. Evergreen Health serves a diverse population, including individuals and families affected by HIV/AIDS, those with other chronic illnesses, and members of the transgender and LGBTQ+ communities. The organization operates multiple care sites and collaborates with community partners to enhance healthcare access and support.

Tabula Rasa HealthCare (TRHC), now part of AnewHealth, is a leader in patient-specific, data-driven technology and solutions aimed at optimizing medication regimens. The company focuses on reducing adverse drug events, hospitalizations, and healthcare costs through integrated clinical pharmacy services and proprietary MedWise® clinical decision support tools. These tools assess pharmacokinetic, pharmacodynamic, and pharmacogenomic risks, enhancing medication safety.

AnewHealth continues to offer a range of services, including comprehensive clinical pharmacy services, national fulfillment and dispensing, and pharmacy benefit management. The company specializes in supporting high-risk patients, such as Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries, and provides pharmacogenomics testing and interpretive services. With a commitment to research and development, AnewHealth aims to advance medication safety and improve health outcomes for at-risk populations through its innovative solutions.

The company emphasizes quality care through rigorous accreditation and partnerships with leading healthcare systems, employing around 913 staff and generating approximately $173.4 million in revenue.

QuadraMed Healthcare Identity Experts is a healthcare software company based in Plano, Texas, founded in 1993. The company specializes in patient and provider identity management solutions aimed at enhancing patient safety, care quality, and operational efficiency.

Its flagship product, the Enterprise Master Patient Index (EMPI), provides features such as accurate patient indexing, data cleanup, duplicate reconciliation, and healthcare data management. The organization operates three health centers and three behavioral health sites, offering a range of services including primary care, dental care, and behavioral health support. BJKPHC is committed to enhancing the quality of life for its community through equitable, trauma-informed healthcare. It collaborates with local health departments and organizations to address public health issues and reduce health disparities in the St. Louis area. With a dedicated staff of 425, BJKPHC also supports workforce development through its scholarship foundation for those pursuing healthcare careers.

Short Description:
LifeLink Foundation is a not-for-profit organization focused on the recovery, processing, and transplantation of organs and tissues to enhance and save lives. Operating primarily in Florida, Georgia, Puerto Rico, and South Carolina, it was established in 1982 and includes five divisions: the LifeLink Tissue Bank, three organ procurement organizations, and a Transplantation Immunology Lab. The foundation is dedicated to honoring organ and tissue donations through compassionate coordination with donor families and healthcare professionals.

LifeLink provides a range of services, including organ procurement and transplantation, tissue recovery and distribution, educational programs, and support services for donor families. The LifeLink Tissue Bank is the largest not-for-profit tissue bank in the Southeastern United States, distributing over 50,000 to 100,000 allografts annually. The organization also engages in research and development to improve transplant outcomes and increase the supply of organs and tissues. LifeLink is accredited by the American Association of Tissue Banks and holds registrations with the FDA and Health Canada.

We believe the heart of our success stems from the dedication and commitment of our professional staff and the enjoyment of working as part of the successful Hart to Heart team. We are proud of our professional staff and reputation, including individuals with extensive experience and certifications, many with 20 to 40 years in the ambulance or transportation industry. Most of our professionals are active members in the volunteer fire companies within their respective communities. We believe this volunteering spirit reflects an enjoyment for their work itself and a dedication to providing the quality service that Hart to Heart is known for.

Coffey Health System is an integral part of the east-central Kansas community. We offer a wide range of services to patients throughout the area. Coffey County Hospital is a 25-bed critical access hospital founded in 1954 in Burlington. CCH earned national accreditation in July of 2017. Our thirteen staff physicians, physician assistants, and nurse practitioners offer unparalleled care in state-of-the-art facilities. Nine of the region's top medical specialists offer outpatient services at Coffey County Hospital.

Our continuum of care also includes CHS Rehabilitation & Wellness Services (speech language pathology and physical/occupational therapy), countywide emergency medical services, and five medical clinics:
Coffey County Medical Center in Burlington,
Gridley Medical Clinic,
LeRoy Medical Clinic,
Waverly Medical Clinic, and
Yates Center Medical Clinic.

When residential care is needed, we offer two services. Coffey County Hospital's long-term care unit (Sunset Manor in Waverly) is recognized for the third consecutive year as a Top Performing Nursing Home by U.S. News & World Reports.

Short Description:
Joslin Diabetes Center is a prominent global institution focused on diabetes research, clinical care, and patient education. Founded in 1898 in Boston by Dr. Elliott P. Joslin, it is affiliated with Harvard Medical School and is recognized as the world's largest freestanding diabetes clinic. The center emphasizes patient empowerment through education and multidisciplinary treatment, aiming to improve diabetes management and reduce complications.

Joslin offers comprehensive diabetes care, including diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of type 1 and type 2 diabetes. Its services feature specialized programs, behavioral health support, and advanced interventions like laser surgery for retinopathy. The center is also a leader in diabetes research, conducting studies on diabetes etiology, genetics, and innovative therapies. Notably, it developed the A1C test, a standard for monitoring blood glucose levels.

Education is a key focus at Joslin, with programs designed to enhance patient knowledge and self-management. The center has a rich history of pioneering advancements in diabetes care and continues to play a vital role in addressing the growing prevalence of diabetes.

Jackson Park Hospital & Medical Center (JPH) is a not-for-profit acute care facility located in a medically underserved area of Chicago's South Side. With a capacity of 256 to 269 beds, JPH serves over one million residents, primarily focusing on a high indigent population, where about 80% of patients are covered by Medicare and Medicaid. Founded around 1920, the hospital has a long history of providing community healthcare, including care for World War I veterans, and has invested significantly in expansions and renovations to meet local needs.

JPH offers a wide range of inpatient and outpatient services, including a 24/7 emergency department, medical and surgical services, and various subspecialties such as cardiology and oncology. The hospital also features a Family Medicine Center, which is the only one with a Family Medicine Residency on the South Side, and provides diagnostic imaging and intensive care services. Committed to medical education, JPH trains physicians through its ACGME-affiliated Family Medicine Residency Program, ensuring a focus on comprehensive care in an urban setting.

The company specializes in developing and managing community-based services, nutritional support, and integrated care solutions for Medicaid, Medicare, dual-eligible, and special needs populations. ILS aims to enhance the quality of life and independence for vulnerable groups through partnerships with health plans, providers, hospitals, and other stakeholders.

ILS offers a range of services tailored to different audiences. For health plans, it provides comprehensive solutions for high-risk populations and managed long-term support services. Short Description:
Guidehealth is an AI-powered health services platform based in Dallas, Texas, founded in 2013. The company focuses on value-based care for health systems, provider networks, and clinically integrated networks (CINs). Guidehealth offers technology-enabled services that enhance primary care workflows by identifying high-risk patients, predicting their needs, coordinating care, and preventing health events. This proactive approach aims to improve care quality and financial performance while reducing administrative burdens.

The platform supports over 500,000 lives across the nation and has secured $14 million in seed funding, including a significant investment from Emory Healthcare. Guidehealth's offerings include AI-powered predictive tools, virtual health guides, and workflow optimization tools. The company has established partnerships with organizations like Koda Health for advance care planning and Story Health for specialized cardiology programs. Key milestones include acquiring Arcadia's value-based care services division in 2023 and forming a strategic partnership with the PA Clinical Network to enhance patient care in Pennsylvania.

Short Description:
Clarest Health is a Virginia-based provider of medication management services, focusing on personalized pharmacy solutions for long-term care facilities and home-based settings. Founded in 2007, the company has grown significantly, now employing over 2,200 people and serving more than 1,300 long-term care locations, impacting over 407,475 patients. Clarest Health aims to enhance patient care through advanced technology and clinical expertise.

The company offers a wide range of services, including long-term care pharmacy services, at-home medication care, and comprehensive medication management solutions. Their PAXIT Med Pass Solution improves medication accuracy and safety, while their Clarest Path program supports health plans in guiding members toward better health outcomes. Clarest Health is committed to delivering unmatched service and data-driven solutions, ensuring that every patient receives individualized care throughout their treatment journey.
